anti_claim: Routing anti-pattern registry rows validate a public navigation failure taxonomy only; they do not authorize route mutation, source mutation, release, provider calls, private-data equivalence, account/session export, or whole-system correctness.

public_private_boundary.public_inputs: public anti-pattern ids, explanatory text, source refs, validator refs, negative-case ids, and copied non-secret macro source-module bodies with hashes and anchors

standard_payload.anti_purpose: A prose-only route warning list that lacks runnable validation, negative cases, source-module manifests, and public/private boundaries.
failure_modes: routing anti-pattern row admitted without stable id or text
routing anti-pattern registry treated as live route mutation authority
private route notes, provider payload bodies, secret values, account/session state, browser/HUD live access, or credential-equivalent material exported through public receipts
source module manifest missing, target body digest stale, or declared anchor absent
receipt embeds copied macro body text instead of carrying public refs, hashes, counts, and verdicts
standard_payload.ontology: A public routing anti-pattern registry organ that validates typed navigation failure rows, negative cases, secret-exclusion policy, and copied non-secret macro registry body.
standard_payload.teleology: Show how the macro system names reusable navigation failure modes without leaking private route material or pretending a registry card can mutate or release work.

validation_rules: The registry must declare kind=routing_anti_patterns and a positive integer version.
Every anti-pattern row must carry a stable unique id and explanatory text.
Exported runtime bundle must include source_module_manifest.json and a copied non-secret macro body module whose target hash and anchors validate.

Private routing bodies, raw voice bodies, provider payload bodies, account/session state, live browser/HUD access, credential material, release authority, provider-call authority, source-mutation authority, route-policy mutation authority, and whole-system correctness claims are rejected.
Receipts must carry refs, hashes, counts, verdicts, and omission boundaries without embedding copied macro body text.
